title: Design for composability in pipelines
description: Read from stdin when no file is given, emit line- or machine-parseable output, and avoid surprises so the tool plays well in pipes.
body: Use when a CLI could be one stage of a pipeline. Do not require interactive input for batch use, force a proprietary output format with no machine-readable option, or buffer the entire stream when streaming would do. Actions: default to reading stdin when no input file is named, support line-oriented or --json output for downstream parsing, stream rather than load-all where possible, and keep output stable across runs. Validation signals include the tool working as a filter in `a | tool | b` and a downstream parser consuming its output. Risk boundary: unstable or human-only output breaks every script depending on it; provide a machine-readable mode and keep its format stable across versions.
